metadata.title: vantage6
metadata.description:
Vantage6 stands for privacy preserving federated learning infrastructure for secure insight exchange.


The project is inspired by the Personal Health Train (PHT) concept. In this analogy vantage6 is the tracks and stations. Compatible algorithms are the trains, and computation tasks are the journey. Vantage6 is completely open source under the Apache License.

What vantage6 does:

delivering algorithms to data stations and collecting their results

managing users, organizations, collaborations, computation tasks and their results

providing control (security) at the data-stations to their owners

The vantage6 infrastructure is designed with three fundamental functional aspects of federated learning.

Autonomy. All involved parties should remain independent and autonomous.

Heterogeneity. Parties should be allowed to have differences in hardware and operating systems.

Flexibility. Related to the latter, a federated learning infrastructure should not limit the use of relevant data.
metadata.notes: If you use this software, please cite it using the metadata from this file.

metadata.title: OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis: v0.5.0
metadata.description:
Changelog for humans

https://openfe-analysis.readthedocs.io/en/latest/CHANGELOG.html#v0-5-0

All PRs included in this release:

  • Add api docs by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/74
  • Change codecov CI to also run on main by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/94
  • Update .readthedocs.yaml by @mikemhenry in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/96
  • [pre-commit.ci] pre-commit autoupdate by @pre-commit-ci[bot] in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/97
  • bump github action versions by @atravitz in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/98
  • Add changelog and rever by @atravitz in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/101
  • Add mypy to CI by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/105
  • Refactor RMSD analyses into MDAnalysis AnaysisBase classes by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/90
  • Handle single frame case in determine_position_indices by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/107
  • Refactor of universe creation and applying transformations by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/93
  • Add plotting utils by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/106
  • Add analysis for symmetry corrected RMSD by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/92
  • Refactor alignment to allow passing through a list of ligands (for SepTop) by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/110
  • Release/v0.5 by @hannahbaumann in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/111

New Contributors

  • @pre-commit-ci[bot] made their first contribution in https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/pull/97

Full Changelog: https://github.com/OpenFreeEnergy/openfe_analysis/compare/v0.4.0...v0.5.0

metadata.title: Analysis of Distributed AI Model Architectures for Cloud-Edge Robotic Systems Utilizing ROS 2 — Reproducibility artifact
metadata.description: This repository contains the complete reproducibility artifact for the comparative evaluation of four ROS 2 deployment patterns (monolithic containers, microservices, dynamic module loading, overlay workspaces) on a K3s cluster pairing an NVIDIA Jetson AGX Orin edge node with two amd64 cloud nodes. It includes the Dockerfiles for the five container images, the four Helm charts, the ROS 2 packages for the camera, YOLO, LLaVA and Voxtral nodes, the automated benchmark scripts, and the aggregated and raw measurement data of the multi-replicate campaign reported in the accompanying article.

metadata.title: Research code for "Targeted interventions can ensure environmental co-benefits from energy decarbonization"
metadata.description:
This repository contains research software for the article **"Targeted interventions can ensure environmental co-benefits from energy decarbonization"**.

It uses scenarios from the [REMIND model](https://github.com/remindmodel/remind) to generate prospective LCA databases, and assesses scenario-wide environmental impacts with [Pathways](https://github.com/polca/pathways).

creators.name: Dürrwächter, Jakob
creators.affiliation: Potsdam Institute for Climate Impact Research

metadata.title: EpiFlow Panel Builder: a spectrally aware reagent-selection tool for multiparametric flow cytometry
metadata.description:
Serrano Lab | Release of V1.2 for Zenodo Publication

Description EpiFlow Panel Builder is a single-page web application for designing spectrally aware flow cytometry panels, built around a curated inventory of 221 reagents from the Serrano Lab at Boston University's Center for Regenerative Medicine. The tool operates one step earlier than spectral viewers: it helps users select which reagents to pull from a lab inventory before instrument setup, flagging detector conflicts and spectral congestion via emission-peak heuristics. It is organized around the 11-marker Core EpiFlow panel for histone H3 post-translational-modification profiling, with layerable biology modules (Neural, Pericyte, Vascular, Cardiac, Immune, NOTCH, and others), and supports the Cytek Aurora 5L and BD FACSDiscover S8. When a panel hits a detector or fluorochrome conflict, the tool suggests same-marker alternatives on different conjugates for one-click swaps.

This release (v1.2) integrates a Round-3 clone-ID verification, raising clone coverage to 205/221 (93%), corrects conjugation and catalog-number errors against vendor product pages, unifies marker labels for the NG2 and Nestin families, and adds a spectral-compatibility disclaimer. Detector assignments are heuristics, not measured spillover; users are responsible for corroborating spectral compatibility in dedicated spectral panel-design software and with single-stained controls.

Author contributions (CRediT): S.S. —  panel design, validation, investigation, software (testing), writing (review and editing). M.A.S. — conceptualization, supervision, resources, funding acquisition, software (architecture and data curation), writing (original draft and review). 

Version v1.2.1

Language English

Keywords flow cytometry; spectral cytometry; panel design; antibody panel; fluorochrome; Cytek Aurora; BD FACSDiscover S8; immunophenotyping; reagent inventory; research software

creators.name: Sreerama, Sandeep
creators.affiliation: Center for Regenerative Medicine, Boston University

creators.name: Serrano, Angie
creators.affiliation: Center for Regenerative Medicine, Boston University

metadata.title: LumpedFlux
metadata.description: LumpedFlux is a software library for specifying and resolving reduced-order lumped parameters (0D) models, especially hemodynamics/cardiovascular.
